titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Makepeace Thackeray Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4116144.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 03:08

did.unittitle: William Makepeace Thackeray collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, and drawings by or related to William Makepeace Thackeray. Correspondence includes letters written by Thackeray to colleagues such as Connop Thirlwall and William Webb Follett Synge, and letters to Thackeray from potential conributors to Cornhill Magazine. Writings include an autograph manuscript of a chapter of The Adventures of Philip, an autograph poem parodying Goethe's Werther, and an autograph manuscript notebook with memoranda, drawings, and quotations from American history, used by Thackeray while writing The Virginians. Also present are sixteen sketches in watercolor and graphite by Thackeray, two groups of ink illustrations for Thackeray's writings by John Priestman Atkinson, and a portrait of Thackeray by an unidentified artist.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and purchase from various sources, 1944-1998.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The William Makepeace Thackeray Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Makepeace Thackeray Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid was produced from a previously existing card set in the Manuscript Catalog. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Uncat MS Vault Thackeray.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, and drawings by or related to William Makepeace Thackeray. Correspondence includes letters written by Thackeray to colleagues such as Connop Thirlwall and William Webb Follett Synge, and letters to Thackeray from potential contributors to The Cornhill Magazine . Writings include an autograph manuscript of a chapter of The Adventures of Philip , and an autograph manuscript notebook with memoranda, drawings, and quotations from American history, used by Thackeray while writing The Virginians . Also present are sixteen sketches in watercolor and graphite by Thackeray, two groups of ink illustrations for Thackeray's writings by John Priestman Atkinson, and a portrait of Thackeray by an unidentified artist.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1846-1862. II. Writings, 1853, undated. III. Drawings, 1859-1878.

persname.part: Goethe, Johann Wolfgang von, 1749-1832 (Werther, Parodies, imitations, etc.)
Synge, W. W. Follett (William Webb Follett), 1826-1891

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains letters written by Thackeray to various colleagues and acquaintances, with one volume of letters written to Thackeray.

did.unittitle: To John Leycester Adolphus; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Ruth Ellen du Pont Lord and Pauline Louise du Pont Harrison, 1970.

did.unittitle: To Charles Bray; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Gordon S. Haight (Yale 1923 BA, 1933 PhD), 1974.

did.unittitle: To Frederic Chapman; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Mary Poindexter Rigby Carlisle, 1955.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Accompanied by a portrait of Thackeray engraved for the Eclectic Magazine .

did.unittitle: To Miss Holmes; autograph letter, signed
To Peter Purcell; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Purchased on the Elizabeth W. Manwaring Fund, 1954.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Accompanied by four leaves and one engraved plate from Notes of a Journey from Cornhill to Grand Cairo (London: Chapman and Hall, 1846).

did.unittitle: To George Augustus Sala; autograph letters (2), signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Purchased from Clive Farahar & Sophie Dupre on the Chauncey Brewster Tinker Prize Fund, 1998.

did.unittitle: To Joseph Rudolph Schachner; autograph letter, signed
To Mr. Scott; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of J. Miller Dixon for the Alan C. Dixon Memorial, 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter written in slanting hand; postscript written in upright hand.

did.unittitle: To William Webb Follett Synge; autograph letter, signed with initials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by an engraved portrait of Thackeray by A. Weger, Liepzig.

did.unittitle: To Thomas James Thackeray; autograph letter, signed

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Mr. and Mrs. W. R. Blum, 1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by an etched portrait of Thackeray by G. Sidney Hunt after Samuel Laurance.

did.unittitle: To Connop Thirlwall, addressed to "My dear Lord Bishop," London; autograph letter, signed
To Thackeray from potential contributors to the The Cornhill Magazine (1 bound volume with 14 autograph letters, signed)

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Julia Millhiser Gimbel, 1973; with bookplate of Jerome Kern.

did.unittitle: Writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains autograph manuscripts by Thackeray, as well as corrected proofs of some of his printed writings.

title.part: The Adventures of Philip on His Way Through the World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript of 2 pages of the novel.

title.part: The Adventures of Philip on His Way Through the World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript, unsigned, of a large part of Chapter XXI (6 pages). Accompanied by a small sheet containing the genealogy of Philip, in Thackeray's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs in various stages of revision, corrected by the author for publication in the The Cornhill Magazine , July-October 1860; bound with two of the articles extracted from the magazine as issued.

did.unittitle: Roundabout Papers , nos. I, III-X.
"Sorrows of Werther"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript notebook with twenty pages of notes, memoranda, and quotations on American history, materials used by Thackeray in writing The Virginians . On the preliminary two fly-leaves Thackeray has written his name and address, "36 Onslow Square, Brompton." The final leaf of the volume has an unfinished verse of poetry and a pencil sketch of Pocahontas rescuing Captain John Smith. On page 14 Thackeray transcribed a letter of his to Messrs. Sampson, Low & Son, referring to The Virginians , dated December 11, 1857. With an armorial bookplate of John and Dorothea Castles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof sheets corrected by the author for publication in the The Cornhill Magazine , January-June 1860; bound.

did.unittitle: Untitled play

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gift of Julia Millhiser Gimbel, 1973; with bookplate of George Barr McCutcheon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript fragment (2 pages) of an unpublished play, with autograph additions and corrections and note by Anne Thackeray Ritchie on front flyleaf; bound.

did.unittitle: Drawings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains illustrations by the British illustrator/wood engraver John Priestman Atkinson, sketches by Thackeray, and a portrait of Thackeray by an unidentified artist.

did.unittitle: Unidentified artist, Portrait of William Makepeace Thackeray; graphite on paper

descriptivenote.p: Unidentified artist, Portrait of William Makepeace Thackeray; graphite on paper

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Related to a drypoint etching by George Barnett Smith.

did.unittitle: Atkinson, J. Priestman, nine illustrations for chapters 3-8 and 10-12 of Cox's Diary ; ink on paper

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Published in The Works of William Makepeace Thackeray ... v. 17 (London : Smith, Elder, 1879).

did.unittitle: Atkinson, J. Priestman, four illustrations for Little Dinner at Timmin's ; ink on paper

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Published in The Works of William Makepeace Thackeray ... v. 12 (London : Smith, Elder, 1879).

did.unittitle: Thackeray, William Makepeace, three sketches; graphite on paper
Thackeray, William Makepeace, thirteen sketches; watercolor and graphite on paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure sketches and still life drawings from Thackeray's time in Bonn and other locations in Germany, with captions and notations.
